findings.title: Potential PII Handling Issues
findings.description: The transition to a consumption-based pricing model may lead to increased collection of personally identifiable information (PII) without proper justification or consent mechanisms in place, violating data handling policies.
assumptions: The new pricing model may require additional data collection from users.

evidence.excerpt: Design and Implement Usage-Based Billing Engine

recommendations: Ensure all data collection is justified and compliant with consent mechanisms.

metadata.mitigation: Implement strict data collection policies and ensure user consent is obtained before any PII is collected.

findings.title: Authentication Weaknesses in New Pricing Model
findings.description: The new billing engine may introduce authentication weaknesses if not properly designed, potentially allowing unauthorized access to sensitive billing information.
assumptions: The new billing engine will require user authentication.

evidence.excerpt: Design and Implement Usage-Based Billing Engine

recommendations: Conduct a security review of the authentication mechanisms in the new billing engine.

metadata.mitigation: Implement robust authentication mechanisms and conduct security testing.

findings.title: Compliance Risk with GDPR and CCPA
findings.description: The new pricing model may lead to compliance risks under GDPR and CCPA if PII is collected without proper consent and retention policies are not adhered to.
assumptions: The new model will involve changes in data handling practices.

evidence.excerpt: Grandfather Existing Customers with 12-Month Price Lock

recommendations: Conduct a legal review to ensure compliance with GDPR and CCPA regarding data collection and retention.

metadata.mitigation: Review and update data handling practices to ensure compliance with applicable regulations.

findings.title: Accessibility Compliance Gaps
findings.description: There may be accessibility compliance gaps in the new pricing model features, particularly in the pricing calculator and cost estimator, which need to meet WCAG AA standards.
assumptions: New features will be developed without considering accessibility from the start.

evidence.excerpt: Build Pricing Calculator and Cost Estimator

recommendations: Ensure all new features are designed with accessibility in mind and undergo WCAG compliance testing.

metadata.mitigation: Incorporate accessibility guidelines into the design and development process.
metadata.policy_rule: Accessibility is a requirement, not an afterthought

summary: Identified 4 risks (1 accessibility, 1 compliance, 1 privacy, 1 security). 1 flagged as blockers. Risk gate: FAILED (1 blockers, 1 warnings).
